chapters.title: HTML Introduction
chapters.desc: HTML is the standard markup language for Web pages. With HTML you can create your own Website.
chapters.fact: HTML stands for Hyper Text Markup Language.
chapters.code: Hello World

chapters.title: HTML Elements
chapters.desc: An HTML element is defined by a start tag, some content, and an end tag.
chapters.fact: The element is everything from the start tag to the end tag.
chapters.code: This is a paragraph.

chapters.title: HTML Attributes
chapters.desc: Attributes provide additional information about elements.
chapters.fact: Attributes are always specified in the start tag.
chapters.code: Visit W3Schools

chapters.title: HTML Headings
chapters.desc: HTML headings are defined with the <h1> to <h6> tags.
chapters.fact: Search engines use headings to index the structure of your web pages.
chapters.code:
Heading 1

Heading 2

chapters.title: HTML Paragraphs
chapters.desc:
The HTML

element defines a paragraph.
chapters.fact: A paragraph always starts on a new line.
chapters.code:
This is a paragraph.

This is another paragraph.

chapters.title: HTML Styles
chapters.desc: The HTML style attribute is used to add styles to an element.
chapters.fact: Setting the style of an HTML element, can be done with the style attribute.
chapters.code:
I am red

I am blue

chapters.title: HTML Formatting
chapters.desc: HTML contains several elements for defining text with a special meaning.
chapters.fact: Formatting elements were designed to display special types of text.
chapters.code: This text is bold This text is italic

chapters.title: HTML CSS
chapters.desc: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets. CSS saves a lot of work. It can control the layout of multiple web pages all at once.
chapters.fact: CSS can be added to HTML elements in 3 ways: Inline, Internal, External.
chapters.code:
<style>
body {background-color: powderblue;}
h1   {color: blue;}
p    {color: red;}
</style>

chapters.title: CSS Introduction
chapters.desc: CSS is the language we use to style a Web page.
chapters.fact: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets.

chapters.title: CSS Syntax
chapters.desc: A CSS rule-set consists of a selector and a declaration block.
chapters.fact: The selector points to the HTML element you want to style.

chapters.title: CSS Selectors
chapters.desc: CSS selectors are used to 'find' (or select) the HTML elements you want to style.
chapters.fact: We can divide CSS selectors into five categories.

chapters.title: CSS Colors
chapters.desc: Colors are specified using predefined color names, or RGB, HEX, HSL, RGBA, HSLA values.
chapters.fact: HTML supports 140 standard color names.
chapters.code:
Hello World

Lorem ipsum...

chapters.title: CSS Backgrounds
chapters.desc: The CSS background properties are used to add background effects for elements.
chapters.fact: You can set backgrounds for individual elements or the entire page.

chapters.title: CSS Box Model
chapters.desc: All HTML elements can be considered as boxes. In CSS, the term 'box model' is used when talking about design and layout.
chapters.fact: The CSS box model is essentially a box that wraps around every HTML element.

chapters.title: JS Introduction
chapters.desc: JavaScript is the world's most popular programming language.
chapters.fact: JavaScript is the programming language of the Web.
chapters.code: console.log('Hello World');

chapters.title: JS Variables
chapters.desc: Variables are containers for storing data.
chapters.fact: In JS, we use var, let, and const to declare variables.

chapters.title: JS Let
chapters.desc: The let keyword was introduced in ES6 (2015).
chapters.fact: Variables defined with let cannot be Redeclared.
chapters.code:
let x = 'John Doe';
// let x = 0; // Error

chapters.title: JS Const
chapters.desc: The const keyword was introduced in ES6 (2015).
chapters.fact: Variables defined with const cannot be Redeclared or Reassigned.

chapters.title: JS Operators
chapters.desc: JavaScript operators are used to assign values, compare values, perform arithmetic operations, and more.
chapters.fact: Arithmetic operators are used to perform arithmetic on numbers.

chapters.title: JS Data Types
chapters.desc: JavaScript variables can hold different data types: numbers, strings, objects and more.
chapters.fact: JavaScript has dynamic types.
chapters.code:
let length = 16;          // Number
let lastName = 'Johnson'; // String
let x = {firstName:'John', lastName:'Doe'}; // Object

chapters.title: JS Functions
chapters.desc: A JavaScript function is a block of code designed to perform a particular task.
chapters.fact: A JavaScript function is executed when 'something' invokes it (calls it).

chapters.title: Python Syntax
chapters.desc: Python syntax can be executed by writing directly in the Command Line.
chapters.fact: Python uses indentation to indicate a block of code.
chapters.code: print('Hello, World!')

chapters.title: Python Comments
chapters.desc: Comments can be used to explain Python code.
chapters.fact: Comments start with a #, and Python will ignore them.
chapters.code:
#This is a comment
print('Hello, World!')

chapters.title: Python Variables
chapters.desc: Variables are containers for storing data values.
chapters.fact: Python has no command for declaring a variable.
chapters.code:
x = 5
y = 'John'
print(x)
print(y)

chapters.title: Python Data Types
chapters.desc: Variables can store data of different types, and different types can do different things.
chapters.fact: Python has many built-in data types.
chapters.code:
x = 5    # int
y = 'Hello' # str
z = 20.5 # float

chapters.title: Python Numbers
chapters.desc: There are three numeric types in Python: int, float, complex.
chapters.fact: Variables of numeric types are created when you assign a value to them.
chapters.code:
x = 1    # int
y = 2.8  # float
z = 1j   # complex

chapters.title: SQL Introduction
chapters.desc: SQL is a standard language for accessing and manipulating databases.
chapters.fact: SQL stands for Structured Query Language.
chapters.code:
-- SQL is case insensitive
SELECT * FROM Customers;

chapters.title: SQL SELECT
chapters.desc: The SELECT statement is used to select data from a database.
chapters.fact: The data returned is stored in a result table, called the result-set.
chapters.code: SELECT CustomerName, City FROM Customers;

chapters.title: SQL DISTINCT
chapters.desc: The SELECT DISTINCT statement is used to return only distinct (different) values.
chapters.fact: Inside a table, a column often contains many duplicate values.
chapters.code: SELECT DISTINCT Country FROM Customers;

chapters.title: SQL WHERE
chapters.desc: The WHERE clause is used to filter records.
chapters.fact: The WHERE clause is used to extract only those records that fulfill a specified condition.
chapters.code:
SELECT * FROM Customers
WHERE Country='Mexico';
